message.content:
{
  "homework": {
    "sessionInformation": {
      "sessionNumber": "1",
      "sessionTitle": "Chemical Substances and Their Properties",
      "subject": "Science (086)",
      "grade": "Class X / 10 / General",
      "difficultyLevel": "Balanced",
      "learningPace": "Balanced",
      "estimatedHomeworkDuration": "45 minutes"
    },
    "homework": [
      {
        "id": 1,
        "type": "MCQ",
        "title": "Identifying Chemical Properties",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["LO_01"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Chemical Substances", "Properties of Matter"],
        "difficulty": "Easy",
        "marks": 2,
        "estimatedTime": "5 minutes",
        "instructions": "Select the correct option that best describes the property mentioned.",
        "question": "Which of the following properties is a physical property of sodium?",
        "options": [
          "It reacts vigorously with water",
          "It has a silvery-white luster and is soft",
          "It burns in air to form an oxide",
          "It displaces hydrogen from dilute acids"
        ],
        "answerSpace": "Write the correct option letter.",
        "visualRequirement": "None",
        "expectedResponse": "B"
      },
      {
        "id": 2,
        "type": "Short Answer",
        "title": "Distinguishing Physical and Chemical Changes",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["LO_01", "LO_02"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Physical Changes", "Chemical Changes"],
        "difficulty": "Medium",
        "marks": 3,
        "estimatedTime": "8 minutes",
        "instructions": "Read the scenarios below and classify them as physical or chemical changes. Provide one reason for each classification.",
        "question": "Classify the following: (a) Melting of ice, (b) Rusting of iron, (c) Burning of a candle wax. Justify your answer.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Write your classification and justification below.",
        "visualRequirement": "None",
        "expectedResponse": "(a) Physical - change in state only; (b) Chemical - new substance (iron oxide) formed; (c) Chemical - combustion produces CO2 and water."
      },
      {
        "id": 3,
        "type": "Diagram Labelling",
        "title": "Reactivity Series Visualization",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["LO_02"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Reactivity of Metals", "Displacement Reactions"],
        "difficulty": "Medium",
        "marks": 4,
        "estimatedTime": "10 minutes",
        "instructions": "Draw a vertical bar chart representing the reactivity series of metals (Potassium, Sodium, Calcium, Magnesium, Aluminum, Zinc, Iron, Copper). Label the most reactive and least reactive metals.",
        "question": "Create a diagram showing the order of reactivity for Potassium, Sodium, Calcium, Magnesium, Aluminum, Zinc, Iron, and Copper. Indicate which metal would displace copper from its salt solution.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Draw the chart and label the metals.",
        "visualRequirement": "A vertical bar chart or list showing reactivity order from top (most reactive) to bottom (least reactive).",
        "expectedResponse": "Diagram showing K > Na > Ca > Mg > Al > Zn > Fe > Cu. Metals above Copper (K, Na, Ca, Mg, Al, Zn, Fe) can displace Copper."
      },
      {
        "id": 4,
        "type": "Numerical / Equation Balancing",
        "title": "Balancing Chemical Equations",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["LO_03"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Chemical Reactions", "Conservation of Mass"],
        "difficulty": "Medium",
        "marks": 4,
        "estimatedTime": "12 minutes",
        "instructions": "Balance the following chemical equations. Ensure the number of atoms of each element is equal on both sides.",
        "question": "Balance the following equations: (a) Mg + O2 -> MgO, (b) Fe + H2O -> Fe3O4 + H2",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Write the balanced equations below.",
        "visualRequirement": "None",
        "expectedResponse": "(a) 2Mg + O2 -> 2MgO; (b) 3Fe + 4H2O -> Fe3O4 + 4H2"
      },
      {
        "id": 5,
        "type": "Case Study / Application",
        "title": "Real-World Corrosion Prevention",
        "learningOutcomeIds": ["LO_04"],
        "topicCoverage": ["Corrosion", "Prevention Methods"],
        "difficulty": "Hard",
        "marks": 5,
        "estimatedTime": "10 minutes",
        "instructions": "Read the scenario and answer the questions based on chemical principles.",
        "question": "A bridge made of iron is showing signs of rusting near the sea. Explain why this happens chemically and suggest two methods to prevent it, explaining the science behind each method.",
        "options": [],
        "answerSpace": "Write your explanation and suggestions.",
        "visualRequirement": "None",
        "expectedResponse": "Explanation: Iron reacts with moisture and oxygen in the air (especially salt water) to form hydrated iron oxide (rust). Prevention: 1. Painting/Greasing - creates a barrier against moisture/oxygen. 2. Galvanization - coating with zinc which acts as a sacrificial anode."
      }
    ],
    "summary": {
      "totalQuestions": 5,
      "totalMarks": 18,
      "estimatedCompletionTime": "45 minutes",
      "learningOutcomesCovered": [
        "Identify physical and chemical properties of substances",
        "Distinguish between physical and chemical changes",
        "Understand reactivity series and displacement reactions",
        "Balance chemical equations correctly"
      ],
      "topicsCovered": [
        "Chemical Substances",
        "Properties of Matter",
        "Physical Changes",
        "Chemical Changes",
        "Reactivity of Metals",
        "Displacement Reactions",
        "Chemical Reactions",
        "Conservation of Mass",
        "Corrosion",
        "Prevention Methods"
      ],
      "subtopicsCovered": [
        "Physical properties vs Chemical properties",
        "Classification of changes",
        "Order of metal reactivity",
        "Balancing equations",
        "Real-world application of corrosion"
      ],
      "taskDistribution": {
        "MCQ": 1,
        "Short Answer": 1,
        "Diagram Labelling": 1,
        "Numerical / Equation Balancing": 1,
        "Case Study / Application": 1
      },
      "homeExperimentIncluded": false,
      "parentEngagementIncluded": false
    }
  }
}
